decisions.rationale: Matches spec Zone 5 definition. Single SOC code input, returns full exposure record with governance metadata. Simple point-lookup pattern — no batch or aggregation at the tool level (clients can call query_table for that).

decisions.decision: Module path: src/mcp_server/ instead of src/mcp/
decisions.rationale: The directory name 'mcp' conflicts with the 'mcp' pip package (Model Context Protocol SDK). Renamed to 'mcp_server' to avoid import shadowing when pythonpath includes src/.

Internal fields (grain hash and promotion timestamp) are not useful for AI agent consumption. The 7 returned fields match the spec: soc_code, occupation_title, exposure_score, stat_res, boss_ai_score, rationale, category.

decisions.decision: Null case returns data=null with descriptive message
decisions.rationale: Per spec: 'If soc_code not found, returns null with a message indicating no AI exposure data is available.' Gemma handles this gracefully in guidance generation.

decisions.decision: Uses query_iceberg_simple (not raw SQL) for data access
decisions.rationale: BaseMCPServer.query_iceberg_simple is the governed query path with audit logging hooks. Avoids raw SQL injection surface.

Exceeds the 50-case minimum. Categories: point_lookup (34), comparison (8), ranking (5), aggregation (7, substitutes for trend since data is static), edge_case (8). All cases include source_table, source_filters, source_column for programmatic verification.

security_notes: All data is read-only — no mutation paths exposed
SOC codes are public BLS identifiers, no PII
Exposure scores are LLM-generated estimates, not proprietary data
